title: Chicken Fajitas

overview.description: Juicy strips of chicken breast with charred peppers and onions, served with warm tortillas and fresh toppings.

method: Mix the cumin, paprika, coriander, oregano, garlic powder, onion powder, cayenne (if using), salt and pepper.
Toss the chicken with the oil, spice mix and lime juice.
Leave to marinate for at least 20 minutes, or up to overnight.
Heat a large frying pan or cast-iron skillet until very hot.
Cook the chicken in batches until lightly charred and just cooked through.
Remove the chicken and keep warm.
Cook the peppers and onion over high heat until softened with some charred edges but still retaining a little bite.
Return the chicken to the pan and toss everything together for 1 minute.

learning.did-you-know: The word 'fajita' originally referred to skirt steak, with chicken becoming popular much later.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to cook quickly over high heat to achieve charred vegetables and juicy chicken.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick family favourite that encourages everyone to build their own wraps using homemade tortillas if available.
chapman-tips: Cook the chicken in batches so it sears instead of steaming.
The vegetables should still have a little bite—they're not meant to be soft.
Warm tortillas are much easier to fold and taste far better.
Finish with a squeeze of fresh lime just before serving.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the cooked chicken and vegetables together for up to 3 months. Warm fresh tortillas when serving.
notes.leftovers: Excellent in quesadillas, rice bowls or salads.
